description: To make a particular change in an environment, practice, timing, or form of support so a stated person can access or participate. The change answers an access relation rather than a supposed diagnosis. 'hinolu' (adapt) can improve any fit; 'hinawu' responds to a person's stated access requirement.
articulatory_notes: Let unvoiced breath pass through the open glottis for 'h', then lift the tongue into high-front unrounded 'i'. Set the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n', carry the voice through the nose, and open into stressed central 'a'. Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', continuing into high-back rounded 'u'.
usage_notes: The object may be the requirement, activity, environment, or process being changed; the affected person may appear with 'wei'. A requested change is one event, and whether it worked is another. Noun use gives an accommodation. An offer leaves consent with the person, and the result still decides whether access followed.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A place remains genuinely shared when its arrangements can change with the people who use it.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The verb makes a requested change discussable without turning assistance into permission to control the person who asked.

semantic_domains.activity: changing a practice so a stated action becomes possible
semantic_domains.community: a particular response to an access requirement
semantic_domains.spatial: altering an environment or route for access
